claims.claim: The semantic stale-window handoff from recovery hold at fill 0.25 to release at fill 0.40 could create a dead zone or jump-cut in continuity.
claims.rationale: The current implementation already blends recovery hold into the selected stale curve over 0.25-0.40 using smoothstep, with tests covering monotonic release and bounded adjacent-step changes.

claims.claim: High-entropy semantic persistence at fill >= 0.55 should make dense thought more stable rather than merely heavier.
claims.rationale: Existing entropy-persistence code and tests verify high entropy extends high-fill semantic anchor retention within the bounded multiplier cap.

claims.claim: A `STALE_SEMANTIC_INTERPOLATION_FACTOR` or equivalent gradual interpolation should replace any binary-adjacent stale-window switch.
claims.rationale: The requested gradual interpolation already exists as smoothstep blending, which avoids a gear-shift feel while keeping live stale-window authority unchanged.
